question: What is the difference between the .env file and env_file in Docker Compose?

options.text: .env substitutes ${VAR} values in docker-compose.yml, while env_file passes variables into the container
No difference — both mechanisms pass variables to the container
options.explanation: These are different mechanisms: .env interpolates in the YAML file, env_file passes variables directly into the container

options.text: .env only works with Docker CLI, env_file only with Compose
options.explanation: docker run --env-file also exists. The difference is that .env in Compose substitutes values in YAML

options.text: env_file is loaded only during build, .env during runtime
options.explanation: .env is substituted when parsing docker-compose.yml, and env_file passes variables when creating the container

question: What does the syntax ${DB_PASSWORD:?DB_PASSWORD is required} mean?

options.text: If DB_PASSWORD is unset or empty, Compose will throw an error with the specified message
Sets DB_PASSWORD to the string 'DB_PASSWORD is required'
options.explanation: The :? syntax throws an error, not sets a value. Use :- for default values

options.text: Creates the variable only if it doesn't exist yet
options.explanation: The :? syntax checks variable presence and throws an error if it's absent or empty

options.text: Encrypts the variable value
options.explanation: Docker Compose doesn't encrypt variables. The :? syntax validates that a variable is required

question: Why shouldn't you store secrets in Dockerfile ARG?

options.text: ARG values are visible through docker history and stored in image metadata
ARG doesn't support special characters in passwords
options.explanation: ARG supports any string values. The problem is that values are saved in image layers and visible to everyone

options.text: ARG automatically logs values to STDOUT
options.explanation: ARG doesn't automatically log values, but they're visible via docker history and docker inspect

options.text: ARG values are shared with all containers on the host
options.explanation: ARG is only available during the build of one image, but its value is preserved in layer history

question: What is the correct priority of environment variables in Docker Compose (highest to lowest)?

options.text: environment in compose.yml → Host shell variables → env_file → .env → ENV in Dockerfile
ENV in Dockerfile → environment in compose.yml → .env → Host shell variables
options.explanation: Dockerfile ENV has the lowest priority. environment in compose.yml and host variables override it

options.text: .env → env_file → environment → Shell variables → ENV in Dockerfile
options.explanation: .env has low priority. Explicit environment values and host shell variables override .env

options.text: All sources have equal priority
options.explanation: There's a clear hierarchy: explicit environment > shell > env_file > .env > Dockerfile ENV

question: How are Docker Secrets more secure than environment variables?

options.text: Secrets are stored in files (/run/secrets/), not in environment variables
Secrets are not visible through docker inspect
Secrets don't leak into logs via console.log(process.env)
Secrets are automatically encrypted during transfer between host and container
options.explanation: In Docker Compose (without Swarm), secrets are passed as bind mounts of files, without additional encryption. Encryption is only available in Docker Swarm
